metadata.campus: The University of Hong Kong – Main Campus / Centennial Campus edge
notes: Expanded beyond the original 10-stop / 25-segment target into a fuller 15-stop / 40-directed-segment graph.
Official building names and map references were normalized against HKU's April 2025 Main Campus map.
Accessible flags were biased toward official lift/ramp routes where HKU explicitly documents them.
Base durations, peak-hour penalties, and popularity remain normalized planning estimates rather than official HKU timing data.

stops.node_description: Primary MTR arrival node for the Main Campus, reached via the lift-only Exit A2 connection on Pokfulam Road.
Base-of-campus engineering and computer-science hub directly linked to the University Street covered walkway and the MTR side.
Centennial-side podium building on the Pokfulam Road edge, commonly used as a transition node between Haking Wong and the Centennial cluster.
Major Centennial Campus tower node connected to the podium system and close to the Jockey Club Tower vertical circulation core.
Upper University Street node between Haking Wong and the Centennial learning-spaces cluster.
Historic academic core of HKU and a key central-campus destination near Sun Yat-sen Place.
Central academic library node; the west-side 2/F entrance connects directly to University Street.
Upper-slope teaching building on the route between the library zone and the science buildings.
Upper academic-zone node near the library and the west-side classroom cluster.
Major Centennial learning hub connected to nearby lecture facilities and the upper covered walkway system.
Large lecture-venue node beside Chi Wah Learning Commons and below the Centennial upper podium level.
Upper-west teaching complex used as a major route anchor toward Bonham Road and the CYM side.
Upper-central academic node connecting the library side, Knowles side, and the CYM/Meng Wah side.
Science-cluster node on the upper slope, east of Run Run Shaw Building and uphill from the library side.
Upper-campus gate node on Bonham Road, commonly used for access to Meng Wah and the west side of Main Campus.
